title: The President of India
explanation: States that there shall be a President of India, establishing the highest constitutional office in the country.
importantPoints: Establishes the head of the Indian State.
Crucial for maintaining constitutional continuity; the office can never remain vacant.
sscImportance: High. Repeatedly asked in SSC Tier-1 as a direct identification question.

title: Executive Power of the Union
explanation: Vests the executive power of the Union in the President, to be exercised directly or through officers subordinate to him.
importantPoints: Makes the President the supreme commander of the Defence Forces of the Union.
Formalizes the executive authority of the Union Government.
sscImportance: High. Frequently tested regarding Supreme Command of Armed Forces.

title: Election of President
explanation: Defines the composition of the Electoral College that elects the President.
importantPoints: Includes elected members of both Houses of Parliament (MPs).
Includes elected members of State Legislative Assemblies (MLAs), plus UTs of Delhi, Puducherry, and J&K.
Excludes nominated members and ALL Legislative Council members (MLCs).
sscImportance: Very High. SSC constantly tests who votes vs who is excluded.
memoryTrick: 54 = 'Electing' 4 groups (LS, RS, Assemblies, UTs) — Elected ONLY.

title: Manner of Election of President
explanation: Lays down the uniform method and proportional representation system for conducting the presidential election.
importantPoints: System of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote.
Voting is conducted by secret ballot.
Ensures parity between Union and States.
sscImportance: Medium. Asked in statement-based Tier-2 questions.

title: Term of Office of President
explanation: Specifies that the President holds office for a period of 5 years from the date of entering office.
importantPoints: Holds office during 5 years or until successor enters office.
Resigns by addressing the Vice-President.
Can be removed via impeachment under Article 61.
sscImportance: High. Addresses to whom resignation is tendered.

title: Eligibility for Re-election
explanation: Declares that a person who holds or has held office as President is eligible for re-election to that office.
importantPoints: No constitutional cap on how many times a person can be re-elected in India (unlike US 2-term limit).
sscImportance: Medium. Direct conceptual question comparing US vs Indian presidency.

title: Qualifications for Election as President
explanation: Outlines eligibility conditions to contest presidential election.
importantPoints: Must be a citizen of India.
Must have completed 35 years of age.
Must be qualified for election as a member of the Lok Sabha.
Must not hold an office of profit.
sscImportance: Very High. Age qualification (35) is an SSC favorite.
memoryTrick: 58 = Quali-58-tions (35 yrs age + LS member eligible).

title: Conditions of President's Office
explanation: Details restrictions and privileges attached to the President's office.
importantPoints: Shall not be a member of either House of Parliament or State Legislature.
Emoluments and allowances cannot be diminished during term of office.
sscImportance: Low. Asked occasionally in multi-statement options.
